summary: ArtCraft (storytold/artcraft) is "the IDE for artists" â€” a Tauri desktop app (Rust backend, crates/desktop/artcraft) plus web app, whose frontend is an Nx TypeScript monorepo (frontend/apps + frontend/libs) built with React 18, Vite, Zustand, Preact signals, Konva (2D canvas), Three.js (3D stage), Tailwind, and FontAwesome Pro. It aggregates a dozen+ image/video models (Nano Banana/Pro, GPT-Image 1/1.5/2, Flux, Seedream, Seedance 1.0â€“2.0, Kling 1.6â€“3.0, Veo 2â€“3.1, Sora 2, Grok, Midjourney, Hunyuan3D, Marble splats) behind a single capability-driven model registry, and wraps them in highly polished "control surfaces": a floating glass prompt box per mode, per-page model+provider pickers, a Konva 2D compositing canvas with inpainting masks, a Three.js 3D blocking stage with multi-camera/focal-length controls, a camera-angle (rotation/tilt/zoom) re-shoot page, a task queue with fake-progress, and a date-grouped drag-out asset library. The architecture is deliberately split so every editor surface lives in a shared lib (libs/components/*) consuming a platform "adapter" interface; the Tauri app and web app supply different adapters (Tauri invoke commands + events vs. REST "OmniGen" API + polling). This makes it an excellent stylistic reference for rebuilding an artcraft-style image/video control experience in plain React+TS.
architecture:
MONOREPO LAYOUT
- Rust workspace at root (crates/) â€” Tauri desktop shell (crates/desktop/artcraft), HTTP monolith (storyteller_web), provider clients (Grok, Sora, Midjourney, Beeble, fal, WorldLabs). The frontend never talks to providers directly; it invokes Tauri commands ("generate_image_command", "generate_video_command", "estimate_video_cost_command", task-queue commands) or REST endpoints.
- frontend/ is an Nx monorepo. apps/: `artcraft` (Tauri desktop frontend, tab-driven shell), `artcraft-webapp` (web SaaS variant with routed pages), `artcraft-website` (marketing), `genhub`. libs/: `model-list` (capability registry), `components/*` (â‰ˆ50 publishable UI libs: promptbox, pagedraw, pagescene, model-selector, gallery-modal, video-editor [504 files â€” full NLE], pricing-modal, popover, slider-v2, tooltip, modal, toaster...), `tauri-api` (typed invoke wrappers), `tauri-events` (typed event hooks), `api` (REST clients), `api-enums`, `common`, `state`.

ADAPTER PATTERN (the key architectural idea worth copying)
Each big editor lib defines a platform adapter interface and receives it as a prop:
- pagedraw/src/lib/adapter.ts â†’ PageDrawAdapter { enqueueEditImage, enqueueInpaint, enqueueBgRemoval, uploadImage, renderBaseImageSelector, onEnqueueMeta }. The Tauri host (apps/artcraft/.../PageDraw/PageDraw.tsx) maps these to GenerateImage invoke calls and bridges Tauri completion events into the lib's Zustand store.
- pagescene's PageSceneAdapter (implemented in useTauriPageSceneAdapter.tsx) covers enqueueGeneration, scene save/load (scene JSON uploaded as a media file + cover image), media listing, CDN URL resolution, uploader modal renderers, viewport size, toasts, logout.
This means the entire control surface (panels, canvas, prompt boxes) is host-agnostic React.

STATE
- Zustand stores per concern: useSceneStore (2D canvas doc), usePromptImageStore / usePromptVideoStore / usePrompt2DStore / usePrompt3DStore / usePromptEditStore (per-mode prompt state, module-global so cost estimators can observe), useClassyModelSelectorStore (selected model per ModelPage + selected provider per model), usePageSceneStore (3D editor UI state: cameras, transform mode, outliner, hotkey gating via DomLevels), useTextToImageStore / useImageToVideoStore (generation batches keyed by subscriberId), useTabStore (active app tab).
- Preact signals for cross-cutting modal visibility (galleryModalVisibleViewMode, galleryModalLightbox*, isActionReminderOpen, authentication).

DATA FLOW FOR A GENERATION (desktop)
1) UI builds a GenerateImageRequest/GenerateVideoRequest with a client-generated `frontend_subscriber_id` (crypto.randomUUID) and `frontend_caller` ("text_to_image", "image_editor", "image_to_video").
2) `invoke("generate_image_command")` â€” the Rust side enqueues with the provider and persists a task in a local SQLite tasks DB.
3) UI optimistically inserts a pending batch/placeholder keyed by subscriberId (HistoryStack tile, batch grid skeleton, TaskQueue row with fake progress driven by per-model `progressBarTime`).
4) Rust emits Tauri events on completion: `text_to_image_generation_complete_event`, `video-generation-complete-event`, `generation-complete-event`, `generation-failed-event`, `canvas bg removed`, credits-changed, etc. Hooks in libs/tauri-events subscribe; each surface filters events by `maybe_frontend_subscriber_id` so it only resolves placeholders it owns.
5) Results carry { media_token, cdn_url, maybe_thumbnail_template } and land simultaneously in (a) the page's local batch UI, (b) the right-rail HistoryStack (2D editor), (c) the TaskQueue completed list, and (d) the persistent media library (server side), browsable via GalleryModal.
WEB VARIANT: artcraft-webapp replaces invoke with an `OmniGenApi` REST call returning `inference_job_token`, then `startVideoPolling` polls JobsApi.GetJobByToken every 4s (max 12 min) â€” and model capabilities come from the server (`OmniGenVideoModelInfo` with duration_seconds_max, aspect_ratio_options, character_references_supported...) instead of the hardcoded model-list.

PERSISTENCE
- Server media library (media_files with Stripe-like tokens, thumbnail URL templates with {WIDTH} placeholder), scenes saved as JSON media files, tasks DB in SQLite on desktop, localStorage for small prefs (enter-to-generate, taskQueueLastReadAt), module-level caches for gallery pages.
domainModel:
MODEL REGISTRY (libs/model-list) â€” the heart of the control surface. Class hierarchy with a `kind` string discriminator (NOT instanceof â€” Vite minification breaks it):
- Model (base): id, tauriId, fullName, category, creator (enum: Grok/Kling/Bytedance/OpenAi/Google/Beeble/Alibaba/BlackForestLabs...with per-creator icons), selectorName, selectorDescription, selectorBadges (e.g. "2 min.", "Preview"), tags (ModelTag: InstructiveEdit | MaskedInpainting | NonMaskedInpainting), providers (GenerationProvider[], default [Artcraft]), progressBarTime (ms for fake progress bar), maxPromptLength (char limit shown as live counter, Infinity allowed).
- ImageModel: maxGenerationCount, defaultGenerationCount, predefinedGenerationCounts (e.g. Grok forces [6], Midjourney [4]), canEditImages, usesInpaintingMask, editingIsInpainting (mask-based edit vs instructive edit), canUseImagePrompt + maxImagePromptCount (1â€“6 reference images), canTextToImage, canEditAngles, canChangeResolution/canChangeAspectRatio (legacy flags), aspectRatios: CommonAspectRatio[], resolutions: CommonResolution[], qualityOptions: CommonQuality[], defaultAspectRatio/defaultResolution/defaultQuality. Helper predicates: supportsNewAspectRatio(), supportsNewResolution(), supportsQuality(), isNanoBananaModel() (enables canvas marker/edit flow), isValidGenerationCount().
- VideoModel: startFrame/endFrame (keyframe support), requiresImage, textToVideoSupported, sizeOptions: SizeOption[] ({tauriValue, textLabel, icon} e.g. 21:9/16:9/4:3/1:1/3:4/9:16 or landscape/portrait/square), supportsCommonAspectRatio, durationOptions (e.g. [3..15]s) + defaultDuration, resolutionOptions (["480p","720p","1080p"]) + defaultResolution, generateWithSound, supportsReferenceMode + maxReferenceImages (9) / maxReferenceVideos (3, maxVideoRefDuration 15s) / maxReferenceAudios (3, maxAudioRefDuration 15s), supportsSystemPrompt.
- SplatModel (Marble world gen), Object3DModel (Hunyuan3D).
ENUMS: CommonAspectRatio (auto, square, square_hd, wide_5x4/4x3/3x2/16x9/21x9, tall_4x5/3x4/2x3/9x16/9x21, legacy "wide"/"tall", auto_2k/3k/4k), CommonResolution (half_k, 480p, 720p, 1k, 1080p, 2k, 3k, 4k), CommonQuality (high/medium/low â€” OpenAI models). NOTE: NO seed and NO denoise-strength parameter anywhere â€” control comes from references, masks, and canvas snapshots instead.
ModelPage enum keys per-surface model selection: text-to-image, image-to-video, canvas-2d, stage-3d, image-editor, image-to-3d-world, image-to-3d-object, angles. Per-page model lists are derived by filtering the registry (canTextToImage â†’ text page; canEditImages|InstructiveEdit â†’ 2D canvas; InstructiveEdit â†’ 3D stage; canEditAngles â†’ angles page).

2D CANVAS DOC (pagedraw SceneState): drawNodes: (Node | LineNode)[] in render order (array order = z-order); Node {id, x, y, width, height, type: rectangle|circle|triangle|image, fill, stroke, rotation, scaleX/Y, offsetX/Y, zIndex, locked, imageUrl/imageFile/imageElement, modelUrl + model3dParams (image nodes baked from a 3D model, re-editable)}; LineNode {points[], stroke, strokeWidth, opacity, globalCompositeOperation (destination-out = eraser)}; inpaintLineNodes kept in a separate Konva layer (the mask); selectedNodeIds[]; clipboard; undo/redo history of serialized snapshots; baseImageInfo (BaseSelectorImage {url, mediaToken, thumbnailUrlTemplate, isBlankCanvas, blankCanvasWidth/Height}) + baseImageBitmap; historyImageBundles: ImageBundle[] ({images: BaseSelectorImage[]}) â€” the right-rail generation history; pendingGenerations: {id: subscriberId, count}[]; aspectRatioType (2:3 / 3:2 / 1:1 â†’ 683/1024-style canvas dims); tool state (activeTool: select|draw|inpaint|eraser|backgroundColor|shape; brushColor/Size/Opacity; inpaintOperation add|minus; inpaintBrushSize).

3D SCENE (pagescene PageSceneStore): cameras: {id, label, focalLength (10â€“200mm), position xyz, rotation xyz, lookAt xyz}[] (max 6, "main" camera undeletable), selectedCameraId, cameraAspectRatio (letterbox: 3:2, 2:3, 1:1, 16:9, 9:16), transform mode (move/rotate/scale) + transformSpace (world/local), outlinerItems, objectPanelCurrent (selected object's vectors), gridVisible, hotkey gating levels (DomLevels.INPUT/DIALOGUE), sceneMeta (title/token/owner).

GENERATION BATCH (text-to-image page): {id=subscriberId, prompt, status pending|complete|failed, images: GeneratedImage[] ({media_token, cdn_url, maybe_thumbnail_template}), requestedCount, modelLabel, modelCreator, failureReason}.

TASKS (TaskQueue via GetTaskQueue invoke): TaskQueueItem {id, task_type, model_type, provider, task_status (pending|started|complete_success|complete_failure|attempt_failed|dead|cancelled_by_*), created_at/updated_at/completed_at, completed_item {primary_media_file {token, cdn_url, maybe_thumbnail_url_template}, media_file_class, maybe_batch_token}, failure_reason {failure_type, failure_message}} â€” failure_type mapped to friendly copy ("rule_bans_user_image_with_faces" â†’ "Images with faces are not allowed").

LIBRARY: GalleryItem {id (media token), label, thumbnail, thumbnailUrlTemplate, fullImage, createdAt, mediaClass (image|video|dimensional), assetType (character|object), batchImageToken, mediaTokens[], imageUrls[]}.

CHARACTERS: StoredCharacter {character_token, name, avatar_image_url} â€” fetched once, used for @-mentions in video prompts (currently Seedance 2.0 only), sent as reference_character_tokens.

ANGLES: AngleConfig {rotation: 0â€“315 in 45Â° steps (displayed âˆ’180..+180), tilt: âˆ’30|0|30|60, zoom: 0|5|10} â†’ sent as adjust_horizontal_angle/adjust_vertical_angle/adjust_zoom on GenerateImageRequest; GeneratedAngle results keep the config that produced them.

featureInventory.feature: Floating glass prompt box (PromptBoxImage / PromptBoxVideo / PromptBox2D / PromptBox3D)
featureInventory.howItWorks: A fixed-position 860px-wide 'glass' (backdrop-blur) rounded-xl card centered at bottom (or vertically centered until first generation, then animated to bottom with react-spring). Contains: auto-growing textarea (rows=1, resize-y, capped via measured viewport math; manual expand/collapse chevron at bottom-center), live character counter 'len / max' bottom-right turning red over model.maxPromptLength, focus ring (ring-1 ring-primary). Below the textarea a toolbar row: LEFT cluster = parameter pickers that render conditionally off model capability flags (aspect ratio, resolution, quality, duration, sound toggle, input mode, camera/grid in 3D); RIGHT cluster = generation count picker + GenerateButton showing estimated credit cost (credits prop from cost estimator, multiplied by count for video). An 'Add Image' icon button toggles the reference strip when model.canUseImagePrompt. Enter-to-generate is an opt-in preference (localStorage) â€” default both Enter and Shift+Enter newline. On submit: builds request, fires invoke, sets isEnqueueing with a 10s failsafe timeout, records prompt+ref thumbnails via window.__storeTaskEnqueueMeta for the task queue.
featureInventory.notes: This is THE signature artcraft control. All four variants share the same skeleton; only the toolbar contents differ per mode.

featureInventory.howItWorks: Hardcoded TS class instances (IMAGE_MODELS, VIDEO_MODELS arrays + BY_ID maps with uniqueness assertion). Every UI control is gated by a model flag: AspectRatioPicker renders only if supportsNewAspectRatio(), duration slider only if durationOptions, sound toggle only if generateWithSound, start/end frame slots only if startFrame/endFrame, reference mode switch only if supportsReferenceMode, quality only for OpenAI models, count picker honors predefinedGenerationCounts. Switching models auto-corrects invalid state via useEffects (reset duration to model default, clamp count, clear end frame if unsupported, exit reference mode).
featureInventory.notes: The webapp version proves the same UI works with server-driven capability metadata (OmniGenVideoModelInfo) â€” for a rebuild, define the registry shape first, source it from server later.

featureInventory.feature: ClassyModelSelector (model picker with nested provider picker)
featureInventory.howItWorks: Bottom-left of every page: label 'Model' + a trigger button showing selected model name with subtitle 'via <provider icon> <provider name>' and a chevron. Opens a PopoverMenu (mode=hoverSelect, max-height 60vh) listing models with creator icons, descriptions, badges (e.g. '2 min.'), 'Soon' disabled rows for unreleased models. Rows for multi-provider models show a trailing provider-icon chip and a hover tooltip submenu 'Select Provider' (Artcraft/Grok/Sora...) with checkmark + left-border highlight on the active one. Selection persists per ModelPage in a Zustand store; provider persists per (page, modelId).

featureInventory.feature: Parameter pickers (AspectRatioPicker, ResolutionPicker, QualityPicker, GenerationCountPicker, duration popover)
featureInventory.howItWorks: All built on a generic PopoverMenu (items with {label, selected, icon, description}, mode='toggle', panelTitle, trigger icon-button with Tooltip). Aspect ratio items use a custom AspectRatioIcon â€” a small rectangle outline drawn at the actual ratio (16:10, 10:16, 1:1, etc.), AutoIcon for auto ratios; labels like '16:9 (Wide)', 'Auto (4K)'. Resolution items: 1K/2K/4K (images) or 480p/720p/1080p (video). Duration is a popover containing a SliderV2 (min/max from model.durationOptions, step 1, suffix 's', debounced 300ms commit, min/max labels under the track) with trigger label like '5s' + clock icon. Sound is a ToggleButton with waveform icon and tooltip 'Sound: ON/OFF'. Count picker constrained by model max/predefined counts.

featureInventory.feature: ImagePromptRow â€” reference media strip (attached above the prompt box, sharing rounded corners)
featureInventory.howItWorks: When visible, the prompt box loses its top rounding and a strip docks above it. Image mode: up to model.maxImagePromptCount thumbnail tiles (56px, glass border, hover X to remove, click to open full preview modal), drag-to-reorder via dnd-kit horizontal sortable, '+' tile opens a chooser (upload file or pick from GalleryModal in select mode), upload progress spinners. Video keyframe mode: distinct labeled 'start frame' (1 image) and optional 'end frame' sections (shown only if model.endFrame). Video reference mode: multi-image (up to 9) + video tiles (up to 3, total â‰¤15s with duration badge '#1 Â· 8s') + audio tiles (play/stop button, duration badge). Tiles map to indexed @-mentions.

When reference media or saved characters exist, the plain textarea is swapped for a contentEditable mention editor. Typing '@' opens an autocomplete (arrow keys/Enter/Tab/Esc) listing @Image1..n, @Video1..n, @Audio1..n with thumbnail previews and @CharacterName with avatars. Mentions are inline-highlighted with deterministic color palettes per type (blues/oranges for images, yellows for videos, violets for audio, teals for characters), the same color as the corresponding tile's index. An '@Characters' toolbar button opens CharactersModal to browse/insert saved characters. On submit, character mentions are regex-extracted (word-boundary, longest-name-first) into reference_character_tokens.
featureInventory.notes: Killer interaction for multi-reference models (Seedance 2.0). Placeholder text teaches it: 'Use @Image1, @Video1, @Audio1... to reference uploads in prompt...'

featureInventory.howItWorks: Full-viewport Konva Stage with a base image layer (the 1024-square 'document', dims from aspectRatioType), a DRAW layer (shapes, images, brush strokes) and a separate INPAINT mask layer. Entry state: a centered 16:9 card with a base-image selector (upload / library / blank canvas). Surrounding chrome is all position:fixed overlays: left tool rail, right HistoryStack, bottom PromptEditor, bottom-left model selector, bottom-right Costs + Help. Interactions: click select; shift/ctrl multi-select toggling; empty-drag = marquee rubber band; per-node Konva Transformer (4 corner anchors, rotateEnabled, keepRatio for images, free ratio for shapes) plus a separate multi-select Transformer (keepRatio) over the combined bounding box; drag moves whole selection (dx/dy applied to all selected); middle-mouse or outside-canvas drag pans the stage; wheel zoom; 'fit' recenters with 40px padding. Z-order = array order with context-menu actions Bring to Front / Bring Forward / Send Backward / Send to Back, plus Duplicate, Delete, Lock, Remove Background. Hotkeys: undo/redo, delete, copy/paste via dedicated hooks. Images dropped from the library land at the drop point via a window CustomEvent ('gallery-2d-drop') with stage-coordinate conversion.
featureInventory.notes: No snap-to-guides in the 2D canvas (snapping exists only in the video-editor timeline). Z-index normalization is a no-op kept for API compat.

featureInventory.howItWorks: The 'Mask' tool only enables when selectedModel.usesInpaintingMask. Activating it shows a top-center floating toolbar: Add/Erase segmented control + brush size SliderV2 (1â€“100pt with +/- steppers, label 'Size 30pt'). Strokes paint translucent mask lines into the dedicated inpaint Konva layer (erase = destination-out). On Generate with an editingIsInpainting model: the inpaint layer is read back via layer.toCanvas at document resolution, encoded to PNG bytes in a Web Worker (maskInWorker), and sent as inpainting_mask_image_raw_bytes alongside the base image media token, prompt, and batch count.

For non-mask edit models, Generate snapshots the visible composition: Konva draw layer read back â†’ ImageBitmap â†’ Web Worker composites it over a clone of the base bitmap at exact document size â†’ PNG File. The bake is pre-computed on idle (300ms debounce after any canvas change, generation-counter to discard stale bakes) so the click path is near-instant. The file is uploaded via PromptsApi.uploadSceneSnapshot â†’ media token â†’ enqueueEditImage with canvas_image_media_token + reference image tokens + aspect/resolution + disableSystemPrompt flag. A generateInFlightRef (not state) guards double-click double-billing.
featureInventory.notes: The 'snapshot the canvas, send as canvas/scene image token' pattern is the unifying generation mechanic across 2D and 3D surfaces.

featureInventory.feature: HistoryStack â€” right-rail generation history (2D canvas & Angles)
featureInventory.howItWorks: An 80px-wide fixed glass column on the right: clock icon header, scrollable stack (max-h 50vh) of square thumbnails grouped into bundles separated by divider lines, newest on top. Pending generations render as N placeholder tiles per batch (count = batch size) with: blurred current base image as background, spinner, and an animated SVG 'running border' (dash marching around the rect). Hovering a pending batch shows red/black diagonal hazard stripes + trash affordance; removal asks confirmation via ActionReminderModal ('may still finish in background and land in your library'). Clicking a completed thumbnail pre-decodes the image then swaps it in as the new base image (selected one gets primary border). Thumbnails use {WIDTH}-templated CDN URLs at 256px. A separate red-bordered Reset All button sits below.
featureInventory.notes: This is how generations 'land back on the canvas': they stack in the rail and one click makes any of them the new editing base.

featureInventory.feature: SideToolbar â€” left tool rail (2D canvas)
featureInventory.howItWorks: Fixed left-center vertical glass rail: Select & Move, Add Shape (flyout: rectangle/circle/triangle + shape color picker that recolors selected shapes live via HsvaColorPicker), Upload Image, Brush (flyout: react-colorful HSVA picker + size + opacity sliders, debounced 75ms), Mask (disabled w/ tooltip 'Model unsupported' unless model supports masks), Eraser, Canvas Background color, divider, Undo, Redo. Active tool highlighted; tooltips on all buttons.

Top-bar icon button (spinner icon while anything is in progress, list-check otherwise) with a count badge (in-progress + unread-completed; unread tracked by completed-IDs diff while popover closed, lastReadAt in localStorage). 400px popover with sections: IN PROGRESS (86px ref-image thumbnail dimmed with spinner overlay and '+N' extra-refs badge, title 'Generating Video...', subtitle 'Model â€” Provider', percent + progress bar + '~ 2m left' â€” all FAKE, computed from created_at against the model's progressBarTime, capped at 95% then 'Almost done...'; dismissable after 5 min), FAILED (red status chip 'Failed'/'Cancelled', human-readable failure reason with tooltip detail, 'Clear failed'), COMPLETED (thumbnail, title, ISO timestamp, click â†’ opens lightbox via signals, 'Clear completed'). Prompt line per row with overflow marquee-on-hover and a copy-prompt button. 'Show all' opens a full modal with the same lists + bulk actions (Clear completed / Clear stale / Clear failed / Nuke all, each confirm-gated). Data: GetTaskQueue invoke polled every 5s + instant refresh on generation-complete/failed Tauri events; prompt/ref-image metadata joined client-side from window.__storeTaskEnqueueMeta records.
featureInventory.notes: The fake progress bar driven by per-model expected duration is a great UX trick â€” honest enough, zero backend cost.

featureInventory.feature: GalleryModal â€” asset library with drag-out
featureInventory.howItWorks: Signal-controlled full modal (view mode) or select mode (used by reference pickers with forceFilter='image', maxSelections, onUseSelected). Items fetched per-user, cached at module level for instant reopen, grouped by date headings, virtualized per date-group with IntersectionObserver (800px margin, measured-height placeholders), skeleton grid while loading. Toolbar: media-class filter popover (All/Image/Video/3D, lockable), grid-density slider (columns 2â€“10), refresh, multi-select with checkboxes + bulk delete (confirm modal), upload button, expand/compress. Items are DRAGGABLE OUT of the modal onto the 2D canvas or 3D scene: GalleryDragComponent renders the drag ghost, the modal hides during drag (galleryModalVisibleDuringDrag signal) and reopens after; drop handlers convert screen â†’ stage/world coords and instantiate an image node / 3D plane / character / object (mediaClass 'dimensional' + assetType 'character'). Clicking opens a Lightbox with prev/next batch navigation (galleryModalLightboxNav signals).

Hero state: huge centered 'Generate Image' title + prompt box vertically centered over a decorative background gallery. After the first enqueue, the prompt box animates (react-spring, tension 200/friction 28) to dock at the bottom and a scrollable feed appears above it: newest batch first, each batch a 4-column grid of square tiles â€” pending tiles are pulsing skeletons; completed tiles are clickable thumbnails opening the lightbox (with cross-batch prev/next); failed batches render one red error tile (reason + Dismiss) padded with empty tiles. Right side of each batch: prompt text card + model badge with creator icon + copy-prompt button. Batches keyed by frontend_subscriber_id; completion arrives via the text_to_image_generation_complete_event.

Same hero pattern ('Generate Video â€” Choose an image, add a prompt, then generate'). PromptBoxVideo with: warning banner when model can't text-to-video ('add a starting frame to animate your prompt'), required-start-frame pulse warning next to Generate, keyframe vs reference input-mode picker (model-gated), duration slider popover, resolution picker, sound toggle, batch count (Seedance 2.0 only, fires N parallel GenerateVideo calls each with its own subscriberId), @Characters. Per-model request quirks switch on tauriId (grok_aspect_ratio, sora_orientation). Results land in the TaskQueue + library (an inline feed exists but is commented out pending backend support for history-by-frontend-id).

featureInventory.feature: 3D Stage (pagescene) â€” scene blocking with cameras
featureInventory.howItWorks: Three.js editor mounted per-tab inside EngineProvider. Chrome: top-center glass toolbar ('+' add-asset popover: ArtCraft Presets (B) / My Library / Upload 3D Model / Upload Image / Upload Splat; magic-wand 'Create 3D model from image' deep-link; Move/Rotate/Scale segmented control (T/R/G hotkeys); World/Local transform-space toggle (X), scale forced local); bottom-left Outliner (scene object list) + PreviewEngineCamera (picture-in-picture of selected camera's view); bottom ControlPanelSceneObject â€” collapsible panel with XYZ position/rotation/scale numeric inputs supporting drag-scrubbing (DraggablePrecisionMutator), color swatch, lock, delete, with undo session semantics (one undo entry per gesture); FocalLengthDisplay overlay while scrubbing; free-fly camera (WASD) + viewport pointer/keyboard hooks; letterbox masks the render to the chosen aspect. PromptBox3D toolbar: model picker, aspect picker (drives BOTH the request param and the 3D letterbox via commonToCameraAspect mapping), resolution, CAMERA picker (list of saved cameras, '+' add up to 6 â€” spawns at randomized orbit position looking at origin, 24mm default; Settings opens CameraSettingsModal), grid toggle, Download frame, Save frame, Generate. CameraSettingsModal: two-pane (camera list | name input + focal length SliderV2 10â€“200mm with mm suffix); while dragging focal length the modal fades to opacity-10 so you see the viewport change live. Generate captures the framed render (snapshotCurrentFrame â†’ File), uploads as scene snapshot â†’ scene_image_media_token + reference images â†’ GenerateImage; result appears in TaskQueue/library.
featureInventory.notes: Multi-camera + focal length + letterbox-synced aspect is the 'camera control' story for stills. Video camera moves are NOT a parameter â€” motion is prompt-described; control comes from start/end frames and references.

featureInventory.feature: Angles page â€” orbit re-shoot of an image
featureInventory.howItWorks: Upload or pick a source image; a draggable 3D 'OrbitSphere' widget plus sliders set rotation (âˆ’180..180 in 45Â° snaps, backend 0â€“315), tilt (âˆ’30/0/30/60) and zoom (0/5/10). Generate sends GenerateImageRequest with adjust_horizontal_angle/adjust_vertical_angle/adjust_zoom + the image token to angle-specialist models (qwen_edit_2511_angles, flux_2_lora_angles, maxImagePromptCount=1). Results stack in the same HistoryStack component; selecting one makes it the new working view. Pending tracked by subscriberId with a module-level Tauri event listener that survives tab switches.
featureInventory.notes: Great example of a purpose-built control surface around a specialist model; the snapped-orbit control is very reusable.

featureInventory.howItWorks: EstimateImageCost/EstimateVideoCost/EstimateSplatCost invoke commands take (model, provider, generation_mode, aspect_ratio, resolution, duration_seconds, batch, generate_audio) and return {cost_in_credits, cost_in_usd_cents, is_free, is_unlimited, is_rate_limited, has_watermark}. useCostBreakdownModalStore keeps estimatedCreditsByPage, observing the prompt stores' current params; the Generate button renders the credit cost inline; bottom-right CostCalculatorButton opens a cost breakdown modal per page; TopBar shows total credits with slow/failed/recovered badge states and retry; failed generations trigger a delayed credits re-fetch (refunds).

A complete timeline NLE lib: multi-track timeline with snapping (element/playhead snap sources), group move/resize, ripple edits, retime/speed, masks, effects, text, stickers, gradients, audio separation, bookmarks, scenes, wasm-accelerated bits, export pipeline, project persistence â€” wired to the host via adapters (asset-gallery, media-source, export-sink, auth, toasts). The library/generation pipeline feeds it assets.
featureInventory.notes: Out of scope for the image/video CONTROL surface goal; treat as separate product. But its timeline/snapping folder structure is a good reference if a timeline is ever needed.

featureInventory.howItWorks: Shot list with per-shot metadata editor (280px left sidebar), durations on a simple time ruler (80px/s), playback transport, sketch-over-canvas (1280x720) with pencil/eraser/undo, upload per shot. Local-only store, feature-flagged off by default.

Signal-driven global confirmation modal (showActionReminder({title, message, primaryActionText, primaryActionIcon, primaryActionBtnClassName, onPrimaryAction})) used for every destructive action: remove pending generation, remove history image, reset all, clear queue sections. Auto-closes if the thing being confirmed resolves itself (e.g. pending generation completes mid-confirm).

featureInventory.feature: Per-surface upload modals + global file drop
featureInventory.howItWorks: UploadModal3D/Image/Splat rendered through adapter slots; GlobalFileDropHandler routes OS file drops to the active surface; uploads go through MediaUploadApi.UploadImage with idempotency UUID â†’ media token, then thumbnail/CDN URLs fetched by token.

aiIntegration:
PROVIDERS & MODELS: GenerationProvider enum â€” Artcraft (their hosted aggregation: fal.ai and first-party routes behind storyteller_web), Grok (xAI consumer client via user's account), Sora (OpenAI consumer), Midjourney, WorldLabs (Marble splats), Beeble (SwitchX relighting). Image models: grok_image, midjourney, flux_pro_1_1(_ultra), flux_1_dev/schnell, nano_banana(_2/_pro), gpt_image_1/1p5/2, seedream_4/4p5/5_lite, qwen_edit_2511_angles, flux_2_lora_angles, flux_pro_kontext_max, flux_pro_inpaint, flux_dev_juggernaut_inpaint. Video: grok_video, grok_imagine_1p5, kling 1.6â†’3.0, happy_horse_1p0 (Alibaba), seedance 1.0 lite/1.5 pro/2.0(/fast), sora_2(_pro), veo_2/3/3.1(+fast), switch_x. Keys/sessions: per-provider credential commands (SetFalApiKey, SetOpenAIApiKey, Sora/Grok/Midjourney credential info, Sora login listener with showActionReminder('soraLogin') â†’ open_sora_login_command â†’ waitForSoraLogin).

ORCHESTRATION (desktop): Frontend invokes generate_image_command / generate_video_command with a normalized request; Rust routes to the provider, persists a task row (SQLite tasks DB), polls/webhooks the provider, downloads results, uploads to the media library (R2/CDN), then emits Tauri events. The frontend's only orchestration duties are: client-generated frontend_subscriber_id correlation, optimistic placeholders, a 10-second 'unblock the Generate button' failsafe timeout (documented hack against hung backends), 5s task-queue polling + event-triggered refresh, and a fake progress bar (elapsed/progressBarTime capped at 95%). Batch video for Seedance 2.0 is achieved client-side by firing N parallel single requests, each with its own subscriberId.

ORCHESTRATION (web): OmniGenApi.generateImage/generateVideo (REST) with idempotency_token: crypto.randomUUID(); server returns inference_job_token; startVideoPolling polls JobsApi.GetJobByToken every 4s (first poll delayed 3s, max 180 attempts â‰ˆ 12 min, backoff x2 on network error); status strings complete_success/fail/dead mapped to UI. Model capability metadata is server-served (OmniGenVideoModelInfo: aspect_ratio_options, duration min/max â€” including a lower max when image references are present (duration_seconds_max_with_image_references), resolution options, character_references_supported), so new models appear without a frontend deploy.

REQUEST SHAPES (the exact parameter surface to port):
GenerateImageRequest: {provider?, model (tauriId string), prompt, aspect_ratio (CommonAspectRatio), resolution (CommonResolution), quality (high|medium|low), batch_size, image_media_tokens[] (reference images, semantics vary per model), canvas_image_media_token | canvas_image_raw_bytes (2D canvas composite â€” becomes first reference), scene_image_media_token | scene_image_raw_bytes (3D render snapshot), inpainting_mask_image_media_token | inpainting_mask_image_raw_bytes (PNG mask bytes), adjust_horizontal_angle / adjust_vertical_angle / adjust_zoom (angle models), enable_system_prompt (bool â€” UI exposes it as a 'system prompt' toggle / disableSystemPrompt), frontend_caller, frontend_subscriber_id, frontend_subscriber_payload}.
GenerateVideoRequest: {provider?, model, prompt, negative_prompt (plumbed but no UI), start_frame_image_media_token, end_frame_image_media_token, reference_image_media_tokens[] (â‰¤9), reference_video_media_tokens[] (â‰¤3, â‰¤15s total), reference_audio_media_tokens[] (â‰¤3, â‰¤15s), reference_character_tokens[] (from @-mentions), aspect_ratio, resolution, duration_seconds, generate_audio, video_batch_count, sora_orientation / grok_aspect_ratio (legacy per-provider), frontend_caller, frontend_subscriber_id}.
Notably ABSENT: seed, guidance/cfg, steps, denoise strength, style presets, camera-move presets, negative-prompt UI. Control is achieved via references, masks, canvas/scene snapshots, keyframes, and angle deltas instead of sampler knobs.

PROMPT CONSTRUCTION: No client-side prompt templates â€” the user prompt is sent verbatim; an optional server-side system prompt can be disabled per request (enable_system_prompt=false when the user toggles 'use system prompt' off, and forced off for models like Seedance where supportsSystemPrompt=false). Character/media grounding is expressed inside the prompt text via @Image1/@Video1/@Audio1/@CharacterName mentions plus parallel token arrays. All UI copy is English already.

EVENTS (libs/tauri-events): text_to_image_generation_complete_event {generated_images: [{media_token, cdn_url, maybe_thumbnail_template}], maybe_frontend_subscriber_id}, video-generation-complete-event {generated_video {cdn_url, media_token}, maybe_frontend_subscriber_id}, generation-enqueue-success/failure, generation-complete-event, generation-failed-event {data: {action: 'generate_image', reason}}, image-edit-complete, object-generation-complete, canvas-bg-removed {media_token, image_cdn_url, maybe_frontend_subscriber_id=nodeId}, credits-balance-changed, subscription-plan-changed, flash-user-input-error / flash-file-download-error (toast bridges), media-file-deleted (plays a sound).

COST: estimate_image/video/splat_cost_command returns credits/USD/is_free/has_watermark per current param combo; surfaced on the Generate button and a per-page cost-breakdown modal.
uiPatterns:
GLOBAL LAYOUT: Custom-chrome desktop window. 56px TopBar: app/tab switcher (MenuIconSelector fed by APP_DESCRIPTORS â€” large icon tiles with image previews and descriptions, 'More' opens an apps quick-menu), scene title input (3D), then right cluster: Upload Images button, Library button (faImages â†’ GalleryModal), TaskQueue popover, credits coin with status badge, settings gear (SettingsModal sections: general/accounts/alerts/about/provider_priority/billing), profile dropdown, window min/max/close. The body is a single tab-switched page (no router on desktop); each page owns a full-viewport surface with floating chrome rather than docked panels.

THE 'FLOATING CHROME OVER CANVAS' PATTERN (signature look): the working surface (Konva stage, Three.js canvas, or hero background) fills the viewport; every control is a position:fixed/absolute 'glass' island â€” `glass` class = translucent panel + backdrop blur + subtle border, rounded-xl/2xl. Standard placements: bottom-center prompt box (860px), left-center vertical tool rail, right-center history rail, top-center mode/tool bar, bottom-left model selector ('Model' + trigger with 'via <provider>'), bottom-right Costs + Help buttons. Overlays toggle pointer-events off during marquee selection so canvas drags don't fight panels.

PROMPT BOX ANATOMY (rebuild spec): [optional reference strip docked flush above, sharing the card's rounded corners (box gets rounded-t-none)] â†’ glass card p-4: textarea row (flex; leading 'Add Image' icon button when supported) with char counter pinned bottom-right; toolbar row mt-2 justify-between: left = capability-gated icon-button pickers each wrapped in Tooltip (popover panels with panelTitle headers and icon lists), right = count picker + GenerateButton (primary color, shows credit estimate, loading spinner state, disabled when prompt empty/requirements unmet); bottom-center chevron expand/collapse tab hanging off the card edge. Inline validation: red pulsing 'Starting frame required' label, info banner for non-T2V models, toast errors for over-length prompts.

POPOVER SYSTEM: one PopoverMenu component powers model pickers, parameter pickers, the task queue, and add-asset menus. Items: {label, description, icon, badges, selected, disabled, trailing, divider, action}; modes: toggle (radio list), hoverSelect (with hover tooltip submenus used for the provider sub-picker), button, default (free content like the duration slider). Panel titles, optional panelAction ('Settings' link in camera picker), showAddButton ('+' in camera picker), maxListHeight 60vh.

CANVAS INTERACTION GRAMMAR (2D): select tool default; click=select, shift-click=toggle multi-select, empty-drag=marquee, drag selected=group move, corner anchors+rotate handle on Transformer (keepRatio for images, free for shapes), multi-select gets a second Transformer around the union bbox; middle-mouse/right-drag/outside-document drag = pan; wheel = zoom; brush/eraser/mask/shape tools take over pointer (cursor preview circle for brush size); context menu (right click) lists Lock, Remove Background, z-order quartet, Duplicate, Delete, greyed appropriately when locked. Document edges clamp drawing (clampToLeftPanel). 'Fit' button recenters/rescales with padding.

3D GRAMMAR: gizmo move/rotate/scale with T/R/G hotkeys and X to flip world/local; free-cam; selectable cameras with PiP preview; aspect letterbox visualizing the output frame; numeric panel with drag-scrub inputs; hotkey gating via DomLevels so typing in inputs/dialogs never triggers editor hotkeys (disableHotkeyInput(DomLevels.INPUT) on focus).

FEEDBACK & MOTION: react-spring for the prompt box centerâ†’bottom docking; pulsing skeletons for pending tiles; animated dash 'running border' SVG on pending history tiles; hover hazard-stripes for batch removal; marquee scroll for overflowing prompt text; toast layer (offset under TopBar); confirmation always via the shared ActionReminderModal; empty states with bouncing callout chips ('Click + to add your first 3D asset!'); badge counters on the queue icon; copy-prompt buttons with check feedback everywhere a prompt is displayed.

THEME: dark UI on CSS variables (--st-*) + Tailwind tokens: bg-ui-background, bg-ui-panel(+border), bg-ui-controls(+border), text-base-fg with opacity steps, brand 'primary' accent, red for destructive, tabular-nums for counters, text-[10px]â€“text-sm scale for control chrome. Thumbnails everywhere use {WIDTH}-templated CDN URLs (256 for rails, 2048/3200 for lightbox).

LIGHTBOX: signal-driven global lightbox with prev/next callbacks injected per context (cross-batch navigation on the image feed), action URLs for download, used from feed, task queue, and library alike.
pitfalls: 1) Dual legacy/new parameter systems everywhere: legacy aspectRatio 'wide|tall|square' + canChangeAspectRatio flags coexist with CommonAspectRatio[] + supportsNewAspectRatio(), legacy SizeOption tauriValues, plus per-provider one-offs (grok_aspect_ratio, sora_orientation). Every picker has two code paths. In a rebuild, define ONE aspect/resolution enum from day one and map per-provider at the API boundary only. 2) Hardcoded client model registry (1,200 lines of TS) â€” the webapp already had to switch to server-driven OmniGen model info; ship server-driven capabilities from the start and keep the class shape as the client-side type. 3) State sprawl: zustand + preact signals + window globals + React context all at once; cross-page coupling via window.__storeTaskEnqueueMeta (prompt/ref metadata for the task queue passed through a window function) and window CustomEvents ('gallery-2d-drop') â€” fine as bridges, but a rebuild should use one event bus/store. 4) Copy-paste drift between the four prompt boxes (PromptBoxImage/Video/2D/3D each reimplement textarea autosize, paste handling, expand logic, char counter; PromptBoxImage even has the same imageMediaId effect duplicated twice in the file). Extract one PromptShell with slots. 5) Hacks acknowledged in comments: 10s setTimeout to force-unblock the Generate button if the backend hangs; fake progress bars capped at 95%; completeBatch falling back to 'most recent pending batch' when subscriber id is missing; auto-fit retry loop polling stage size 20 times. 6) Konva/Vite gotcha: instanceof breaks under minified class names â€” they use `kind` string discriminators on Model classes; keep that. Same for stale-closure traps: many useEffects deliberately read zustand getState() instead of deps; the generation-counter guards (baseImageLoadGen, bake generation counter) exist to kill races from rapid history navigation â€” these are real bugs you will hit, copy the guards. 7) zIndex is stored on Node but IGNORED in PageDraw (array order wins); normalizeZIndices is a no-op kept for API compat â€” pick one z-order representation. 8) Per-(page,model) provider selection is admitted to be wrongly keyed ('TODO: shouldn't be per-page'). 9) Tab-switch = full unmount of the 3D engine (intentional but means scene state must serialize constantly); module-level caches and module-level Tauri listeners (AnglesStore) are used to survive remounts â€” document which state must outlive the page. 10) No seed/strength controls means no reproducibility story; if you want seeds, add them to the registry capability model rather than bolting on per-model UI. 11) Mixed fetch strategies (FetchProxy vs fetch vs LoadWithoutCors CORS fallback) and CORS cache-busting query params on history thumbnails hint at CORS-tainted-canvas pain â€” plan crossOrigin/anonymous + CDN CORS headers up front since canvas readback (composite bake) requires untainted images. 12) Some dead/disabled code shipped (commented-out video history feed waiting on backend, Seedance alpha warning, ImageStyleSelector with weight field that renders only a hidden input) â€” the 'weight per style image' concept exists in types but was never finished.
